commit.message:
Tighten IntervalSelection scopes and clear build warnings

Refine the thin-interval selection file so the generic subobject-cardinality and lift-subobject helper blocks sit outside the triangulated scope, while the genuinely strict/triangulated statements stay inside the local `[IsTriangulated C]` section.

The remaining proof edits are local linter cleanups:
- remove dead `change` steps in the inclusion transport arguments
- replace wrapper `simpa` proofs by direct exact rewrites or goal-local `simp`
- make the lifted-subobject and lifted-cokernel comparison proofs explicit at the arrow level
- keep the maximal/minimal phase selection statements unchanged

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.IntervalSelection
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Deformation.IntervalSelection

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean MDQ helper scopes and build-time linter warnings

Refine the MDQ helper layer so the generic `Subobject` transport lemmas no longer inherit the ambient `[Abelian A]` unnecessarily, while keeping the cokernel-facing API in the abelian scope where it belongs.

The remaining proof edits are local cleanups rather than argument changes:
- replace a handful of unnecessary `simpa` steps with direct rewrites or `simp`
- remove stale simp arguments in the subobject transport proofs
- keep the mdq/kernel recursion and appended-filtration arguments mathematically unchanged

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.StabilityFunction.MDQ
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.StabilityFunction.MDQ

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean unnecessary simpa in StrictShortExactSequence

Replace the final `simpa [hzero]` in the pullback-cokernel isZero step with the direct rewrite-and-simplify form the linter expects.

The proof shape does not change: it still derives the vanishing of the identity by cancelling the strict-epi pullback map after establishing `hzero`. This only removes the local build-time linter warning in `BridgelandStability.Deformation.StrictShortExactSequence`.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.StrictShortExactSequence
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Deformation.StrictShortExactSequence

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighten triangulated scope in FirstStrictSES

Clean the remaining build-time warning in FirstStrictSES and finish the
ongoing interface cleanup around the first strict short exact sequence.

This pass:
- removes the redundant ambient `IsTriangulated` variable from the generic
  semistability transport lemma
- groups the genuinely triangulated arguments into a local section instead of
  repeating `variable ... in` before each theorem
- drops unused theorem arguments from the maximal-phase comparison lemma and
  updates the downstream call site
- replaces the last local `simpa` in the zero-identity argument with an
  explicit rewrite by `hzero`

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.FirstStrictSES
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Deformation.FirstStrictSES

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean numerical-manifold transport proofs

Remove the remaining build-time warning noise in NumericalStabilityManifold
by making two local transport steps explicit.

This pass:
- rewrites the range-membership step in the finite-dimensionality proof
  directly, instead of wrapping it in `simpa`
- factors out the component-representative equality in the openness proof and
  uses it through an explicit iff proof instead of a value-based `simpa`

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.NumericalStabilityManifold
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.NumericalStabilityManifold

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Remove stale longFile override from StabilityCondition.Basic

StabilityCondition.Basic no longer exceeds the default longFile threshold, so
its local override is just repeated build noise. Remove it and keep the file
on the standard linter setting.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.Basic
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.Basic

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighten interval finite-length transport lemmas

Clean the remaining build-time warnings in IntervalCategory.FiniteLength and
finish the local assumption-scope cleanup around the left-heart short exact
sequence theorem.

This pass:
- removes dead `Category.assoc` simp arguments
- replaces two transport `simpa` steps with direct rewrites
- moves the `(a, b)` assumptions for the left-heart extension theorem into a
  shared section and omits the unused `Fact (a < b)` from its interface

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.FiniteLength
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.FiniteLength

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean strictness transport proofs in IntervalCategory

Remove the remaining build-time linter noise in IntervalCategory.Strictness
without changing the strictness arguments.

This pass:
- replaces local `simpa` wrappers with explicit rewrites in the right-heart
  mono proof
- switches the deprecated `Cones.postcompose` name to `Cone.postcompose`
- makes the comparison-isomorphism identities explicit at the underlying
  induced-category level
- drops dead `Category.assoc` simp arguments in the cokernel transport proof

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.Strictness
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.Strictness

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refine interval quasi-abelian kernel and cokernel helpers

Clean the local build-time warnings in IntervalCategory.QuasiAbelian and
finish the interface cleanup that was already in progress.

This pass:
- changes the existence wrappers for interval kernels and cokernels from
  `def` to `theorem`, since they only package typeclass evidence
- replaces the four induced-category `simpa` wrappers with direct underlying
  equalities for the comparison isomorphisms

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.QuasiAbelian
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.QuasiAbelian

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighten heart-embedding interfaces in TwoHeartEmbedding

Clean the local build-time warnings in TwoHeartEmbedding and tighten the
assumption scope around the thin-interval heart transfer lemmas.

This pass:
- drops unused binder names in the two full instances
- makes the induced-category isomorphism identities explicit without `simpa`
- moves the `(a, b)` and `Fact (a < b)` assumptions to a shared local section
  and omits the unused `Fact (b - a ≤ 1)` from the theorem interfaces

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.TwoHeartEmbedding
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.TwoHeartEmbedding

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean arithmetic linter noise in TStructureConstruction

Simplify the boundedness proof in Slicing.TStructureConstruction so the
build-time linter stops flagging the local arithmetic block.

This removes a dead `push_cast`, replaces the wrapped ceil bound with a
direct `linarith` input, and uses `omega` for the integer inequality behind
`Int.lt_ceil`.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Slicing.TStructureConstruction
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Slicing.TStructureConstruction

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Remove stale longFile override from Slicing.Basic

Slicing.Basic no longer exceeds the default longFile threshold, so the local
override is now just linter noise. Remove it and keep the file on the
standard setting.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Slicing.Basic
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Slicing.Basic

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Mark heart abelianity wrappers as reducible

The heart abelianity helpers in HeartAbelian.lean are class-valued wrapper
definitions. Mark them reducible so the build-time class-definition linter
stops flagging them.

This is purely an interface cleanup; the underlying constructions are
unchanged.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.TStructure.HeartAbelian
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.TStructure.HeartAbelian

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean build-time warnings in IntervalCategory.Basic

Remove the remaining local build-time warnings in IntervalCategory.Basic
without changing the interval-category arguments.

This pass:
- makes the induced-category isomorphism identities explicit at the
  underlying morphism level
- drops an unused binder from the interval inclusion functor
- removes a dead `Category.assoc` simp argument in the retract proof

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.Basic
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.IntervalCategory.Basic

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Clean build-time linter warnings in Strict

Tighten Strict.lean so it compiles without the local build-time linter
warnings that were still firing even though runLinter passed.

This pass keeps the arguments intact and focuses on the warning sources:
- replace vacuous `simpa` / `change` patterns with explicit reassociation or
  exact proofs
- mark the class-valued `normalEpi` and `normalMono` wrappers as reducible
- narrow `Preadditive` scope where later declarations no longer depend on it
- keep the subobject-transfer proofs stable while removing dead tactic noise

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Strict
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Strict

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve PackageMaker manual (#73)

Expand the manual into a tutorial-first guide with a complete
wizard walkthrough, explanations of key choices, a generated-file
tour, and a concrete next-steps checklist for new package authors.

Tighten the README so it stays a quickstart while pointing readers
to the manual and the generated makedoc.g script for the fuller
workflow documentation.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: PackageMaker
repository.description: A simple GAP script for creating a GAP package skeleton

commit.message:
Clean H0Functor docs and remove stale docBlame suppression

The local docBlame suppression in H0Functor was the wrong fix. The file
now lints cleanly without it, so drop the nolint block instead of carrying
an unexplained exception.

Also simplify the final step of
HeartStabilityData.toH0primeHom_fromH0primeHom_of_isGE by passing the
comparison equality in the orientation expected by toH0primeHom_eq.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Functor
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Functor

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighten H0Homological interfaces and document shift constructions

Document the truncation/shift comparison constructions and the spectral-object map introduced in the H0 homological development.

Remove duplicated explicit triangulated binders from declarations whose ambient section already supplies the instance, trim one unused `Category.assoc` simp argument, and drop the unused `hm₁` / `IsGE` hypotheses from the exactness lemmas.

For the three remaining linter blockers, make the triangulated dependence explicit in the theorem types by threading the instance through the `H0prime` and `H0Functor` terms directly instead of relying on an anonymous ambient instance. This keeps the API honest and satisfies `unusedArguments` without changing the mathematics.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Homological
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Homological

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ui): restore Seraph scan sweep

Summary:
- keep the Seraph scanline inside the visualization stage above the footer
- make the sweep travel top to bottom across the full stage
- simplify the active workspace window border treatment

Rationale:
- the scanline was stopping mid-stage and then sweeping in the wrong direction
- the selected window chrome remained too stacked and noisy

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/seraphPresence.test.ts src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(symphony): serialize users cache fill for concurrent lookups

Summary:
- replace the users cache mutex/option with an async OnceCell so concurrent
  assignee lookups share a single users-list fetch
- add a concurrent regression test asserting two simultaneous candidate fetches
  trigger only one `SymphonyLinearListUsers` request

Rationale:
- prevents duplicate users pagination bursts during startup races
- lowers unnecessary Linear API pressure while preserving session cache behavior

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo fmt
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test --test linear_client_tests
  test_fetch_candidates_with_ambiguous_assignee_errors -- --nocapture
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test --test linear_client_tests
  test_fetch_candidates_named_assignee_concurrent_requests_share_users_fetch
  -- --nocapture
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(symphony): fail on ambiguous assignee matches

Summary:
- changed named assignee resolution to collect all matching user IDs and
  return an explicit ambiguity error when multiple users match
- kept cache writes limited to exact single-user matches only
- added regression coverage for ambiguous name collisions

Rationale:
- prevents silently routing work to an arbitrary user when names are not
  unique across Linear accounts

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighten H0Functor interfaces and suppress verified docBlame false positives

Refine the H0Functor layer so its helper lemmas and constructions expose
only the assumptions they actually use, and document the H0prime bridge
constructions while working around a linter false positive in a narrow,
verified way.

- move the truncGE/descTruncGE helper block into an omitted
  IsTriangulated section so those lemmas no longer export a hidden
  triangulated assumption
- remove the ambient IsTriangulated binder from the H0prime mapping,
  comparison, and naturality constructions whose proofs only rely on the
  smaller truncation API
- add docstrings to the core H0prime bridge constructions
- suppress docBlame only for the five affected public noncomputable defs
  after verifying with findDocString? and hover that the docstrings are
  present in the environment; this is a linter false positive rather than
  missing documentation

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Functor
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.H0Functor

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

perf(ui): isolate Seraph footer ticks

Summary:
- keep the current Seraph visuals while moving the 30ms footer ticker into a tiny child component
- stop the main presence animation tree from rerendering on every footer tick

Rationale:
- the wing/core motion should stay reference-accurate without paying for a full component rerender loop
- isolating the ticker is the lowest-risk CPU reduction for the current animation path

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test
- cd frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Tighten HeartEquivalence.Basic theorem interfaces

Narrow the exported statements in HeartEquivalence.Basic so the file
stops leaking ambient triangulated assumptions into declarations that do
not actually need them.

- generalize FullSubcategory.isZero_of_obj_isZero to an explicit minimal
  category context, including the zero-morphism structure it actually
  uses, instead of inheriting unused section variables
- keep heartFullSubcategory_shortExact_triangle under an omitted
  IsTriangulated binder without reintroducing the heart's abelian
  instance into the theorem interface
- omit the ambient triangulated instance from the K0 shift lemmas so the
  linter sees their actual dependency surface

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.Basic
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.HeartEquivalence.Basic

- live-changing activity already belongs in Operator Timeline and Live Trace

fix(symphony): address assignee resolver review feedback

Summary:
- clarified unresolved assignee errors to report available names/emails
- tightened users-cache return logic to remove an unreachable fallback path
- normalized UUID assignee input to lowercase before passthrough matching
- added regression coverage for users-query pagination and uppercase UUID
  passthrough behavior

Rationale:

  acceptance behavior and startup failure clarity

Tests:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Normalize deployed page title

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

Narrow numerical manifold interfaces to their actual dependencies

This pass removes the remaining linter failures in `NumericalStabilityManifold` by tightening three declaration interfaces rather than changing any mathematical content.

First, the factoring lemma and the finite-dimensionality theorem are moved into declarations with explicitly reduced categorical assumptions, so they no longer inherit a phantom `IsTriangulated` argument from the file-level variable scope. Their statements only use the algebra of `K₀`, the numerical quotient, and additive finite generation, so the theorem interfaces now reflect that.

Second, the generic charted-space construction theorem now asks only for the topological structure it actually uses. The target model already carries a topology through `NormedAddCommGroup`, and the proof never touches the `NormedSpace ℂ E` structure, so the redundant class argument has been removed.

Verified with:
- `lake build BridgelandStability.NumericalStabilityManifold`
- `lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.NumericalStabilityManifold`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refine StabilityCondition local model and trim hidden triangulated dependencies

This pass cleans the remaining linter issues in the StabilityCondition topology cluster without broad proof churn.

In `LocalHomeomorphism`, inline the normed-space core into the instance and add docstrings to the component local-model interface so the exported API is documented directly where it is consumed.

In `Topology` and `ConnectedComponent`, narrow theorem scopes instead of threading phantom `IsTriangulated` parameters through otherwise pretriangulated statements. The phase-bound helpers and the `K₀` vanishing lemma now live in smaller local sections that declare only the structure they actually use, and Lemma 6.4 is restated in a section that contributes exactly one triangulated instance via `StabilityCondition` rather than inheriting a duplicate hidden argument from the file-wide scope.

Verified with:
- `lake build BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.Topology`
- `lake build BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.ConnectedComponent`
- `lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.Topology BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.ConnectedComponent BridgelandStability.StabilityCondition.LocalHomeomorphism`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- remove the double-framed active pane treatment
- keep selection obvious with a cleaner border and top accent line

Rationale:
- the previous active state was easy to spot but looked stacked and noisy
- the workspace needs a more precise focus treatment before further cockpit polish

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test
- cd frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(symphony): resolve tracker assignee names and emails

Summary:
- extended assignee resolution to keep `me`, pass through lowercase UUIDs,
  and resolve non-UUID values by querying Linear users
- added session-lifetime caches for resolved assignee IDs and fetched user
  lists to avoid repeated lookup calls
- added regression coverage for username/email resolution, UUID passthrough,
  unresolved-name startup errors, and cache reuse

Rationale:
- tracker assignee filters should accept human-friendly identifiers instead
  of requiring users to manually discover UUIDs
- unresolved assignee values now fail early with available usernames to make
  startup misconfiguration obvious and actionable

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test --test linear_client_tests test_fetch_candidates_with_ -- --nocapture
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test --test linear_client_tests test_fetch_candidates_named_assignee_uses_session_cache -- --nocapture
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

deps: re-sign bundled zstd on macOS (#61355)

Rewriting the bundled zstd executables with install_name_tool
invalidates their existing signatures on macOS. Re-sign them ad
hoc after updating the rpath so the CLI remains launchable for Pkg
archive decompression.

Add a regression test that runs the bundled zstd executable
instead of only checking that libzstd can be loaded.

Fixes #61354 (but see discussion there as to whether it really does?)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: julia
repository.description: The Julia Programming Language

commit.message:
Repair deformation theorem downstream of reduced deformed-predicate APIs

This pass restores the main deformation theorem file after the earlier cleanup to the deformed predicate interfaces, and it closes the remaining hidden-instance lint issues in the deformation theorem boundary.

In DeformedSlicing, the two exported theorems were redundantly wrapped in an extra  binder even though the file already carries that instance globally. Removing that duplicate wrapper fixes the exported theorem types without touching the proofs.

In Theorem, the downstream breakage was real: the file was still calling the old deformedGtPred/deformedLtPred arities, which turned a routine API migration into build failures in the umbrella target. Those calls are now updated to the reduced interfaces. The two Bridgeland 7.1 theorems also now make their dependence on the constructed deformed slicing explicit in the theorem type, which keeps the linter from manufacturing a hidden unused triangulated-instance argument.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.DeformedSlicing BridgelandStability.Deformation.Theorem
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Deformation.DeformedSlicing BridgelandStability.Deformation.Theorem
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.Theorem

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Tighter rules for referencing (#2678)

Classify @ref targets by syntax before resolver dispatch, keep
header labels and @id targets ahead of docstrings, support explicit
backticked docrefs, and update code-shaped explicit refs to use
backticks.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: Documenter.jl
repository.description: A documentation generator for Julia.

commit.message:
Tighten deformed slicing HN interfaces and clarify IntervalAbelian dependencies

This pass cleans the remaining focused linter issues in the deformed slicing/interval-abelian boundary without introducing local linter suppressions.

In DeformedSlicingHN, the shift and triangle-closure lemmas now use the reduced deformed-predicate interfaces directly. The stale hε2/hsin parameters have been removed from theorem signatures and downstream call sites, so the exported API now matches the underlying predicate definitions instead of carrying historical baggage.

In IntervalAbelian, the two Lemma 5.2 theorems were still picking up a hidden IsTriangulated argument. The fix here is not a nolint workaround: the theorem types now make the dependence on Slicing.toTStructure explicit through a local let-bound TStructure built with the ambient triangulated instance. That keeps the statements honest and eliminates the hidden-argument linter noise.

Verification:
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.IntervalAbelian
- lake build BridgelandStability.Deformation.DeformedSlicingHN
- lake exe runLinter BridgelandStability.Deformation.DeformedSlicingHN BridgelandStability.Deformation.IntervalAbelian

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(symphony): harden workflow publish-gate regression test

Summary:
- anchor the workflow test path to CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR to avoid
  working-directory dependent failures
- assert on the newly added publish-gate blocking phrase so the test guards
  the exact contract introduced in this ticket

Rationale:
- PR bot feedback correctly identified that the prior assertion could pass on
  pre-existing wording and that the relative path was brittle

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cd apps/symphony && cargo fmt -- --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(symphony): enforce publish proofs before Human Review

Summary:
- require explicit publish-proof commands in WORKFLOW.md before moving to
  Human Review
- mirror the same publish-proof requirements in WORKFLOW-REFERENCE.md
- add a workflow contract test to prevent regressions in publish-gate wording

Rationale:
- the prior prompt relied on checklist intent and allowed sessions to advance
  without concrete command evidence that branch/PR publication succeeded
- explicit command-level proofs and a regression test tighten the completion
  bar so review transition is blocked until publish prerequisites are verified

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cd apps/symphony && cargo fmt -- --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): adopt calver and refine Seraph surface

Summary:
- switch the app versioning to the dated v2026.3.19 release line and expose a shared SERAPH_PRIME build id in the frontend
- refine the Seraph presence surface with a full-stage scan treatment, cleaner pane framing, and less user-facing cockpit wording
- align the live workspace copy so visible labels favor workspace/live language over cockpit branding

Rationale:
- cut the March 19 release identity the user requested using the repo's existing CalVer convention

commit.message:
docs(symphony): route Todo PR feedback through Agent Review

Summary:
- Updated the Todo special-case status-map text to explicitly move issues
  into Agent Review when a PR is already attached.
- Kept the feedback sweep requirements and return-to-Human-Review path
  unchanged while making the state transition explicit.

Rationale:
- A reviewer noted the previous wording allowed feedback handling without
  ever entering Agent Review, which weakens state-history clarity.

Tests:
- rg -n "active_states|Agent Review|Step 0|Rework|Human Review|PR feedback sweep" apps/symphony/WORKFLOW.md
- rg -n "review feedback requires changes|submit for review|move to Human Review" apps/symphony/WORKFLOW.md

docs(symphony): define Agent Review as PR feedback loop

Summary:
- Clarified status-map semantics so Rework is only for reset-level
  rejection of the current approach.
- Added an explicit full lifecycle flow that routes review-requested
  changes through Agent Review and back to Human Review.
- Updated Human Review handling to send actionable feedback to
  Agent Review and reserve Rework for full resets.

Rationale:
- The workflow needed a distinct PR feedback state that keeps work on
  the same branch/PR instead of forcing a restart.
- Rework remains a full reset path for rejected approaches, matching
  the intended state model.

Tests:
- rg -n "active_states|Agent Review|Step 0|Rework|Human Review|PR feedback sweep" apps/symphony/WORKFLOW.md
- rg -n "review feedback requires changes|submit for review|move to Human Review" apps/symphony/WORKFLOW.md

refactor(api): unify slave-centric runtime surface

Summary:
- collapse the normal runtime API into EtherCAT and remove
  EtherCAT.Device from the public surface
- switch the public read/write/event model to driver-backed slave
  snapshots, subscriptions, and commands
- reduce EtherCAT.Driver to the core runtime callbacks and move
  provisioning, latch, and simulator hooks into specialist modules
- simplify runtime projection, retained output staging, and simulator
  support around the new boundary

Rationale:
- make the public API match the runtime's real ownership model of
  named EtherCAT slaves
- remove overlapping signal-image and device abstractions that made
  the library harder to reason about and harder for Ogol to bind to
- keep driver authors on a smaller, more focused extension contract
  while preserving specialist hooks where they actually belong

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test --max-failures 1
- (../ogol) mix test

Codex 用の環境設定ファイルを追加し、セットアップ手順を定義する。\n\nCo-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Implement hierarchical group and frame selection rules

Enforce the ancestor/descendant selection invariant in BoardSelection so selection never contains both a container and one of its descendants. Canvas selection now keeps clicks on nested items on the exact item, treats frames as directly selectable, and keeps groups non-visual by filtering them out of pointer hit-testing and canvas rectangle selection.

Add reusable hierarchy navigation APIs for UI consumers, including parent lookup, parent chains, hierarchy paths, common-parent promotion, and ancestor selection by id. Also preserve container subtrees during reparenting by allowing index removals that do not implicitly detach children, which fixes nested group/frame movement and navigation state.

Cover the new semantics with end-to-end tests for click selection, multi-selection normalization, drag behavior, group bounds, frame resize semantics, and parent navigation, and update the existing group regression to reflect child-first click behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Adopt Lean 4 module system across the project

Migrate the BridgelandStability project to Lean 4's current module system. Every project file now starts with `module`, the existing outward-facing import surface is expressed with `public import`, and each file's declarations live under a `public section` so the repo no longer relies on the pre-module-system default export behavior.

To keep the recent file splits working under the new visibility rules, add the temporary compatibility options `backward.privateInPublic` and `backward.proofsInPublic` in the project modules. This preserves the current API shape while making the codebase build on the module system today, rather than forcing an immediate rewrite of every split proof cluster.

Also add one targeted `import all` in `HeartEquivalence/EulerLift` for `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.TStructure.TruncLTGE`, where a proof was unfolding non-exposed Mathlib internals. That isolates the one known cross-package visibility escape hatch instead of widening exposure more broadly.

Verified with `lake build BridgelandStability`.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: github-digest更新でstability-daysを適用しない

GitHub Actionsのdigest更新でminimumReleaseAgeの判定が解除されず、renovate/stability-daysがpendingのままになる問題を避けるため、github-digest datasourceを待機対象から除外する。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Add extern declarations (extern fn / extern struct) throughout the
compiler and an injected standard prelude with std APIs: print, println,
to_str, str_concat, str_len, str_free.
* Provide a packaged std runtime library and runtime/std path resolution
utilities.

* **Bug Fixes**
* Better parse/type error formatting with accurate file:line locations
and safe prelude-injection avoidance.

* **Chores**
* Build graph updated to include library, resource-path, and CLI-support
components.

* **Tests**
* Add parser, lowering, LIR, codegen, and integration tests covering
externs and the prelude.

Summary:

  mono_tum_vi, including bounded frame diagnostics and save-skip
  execution support
- teach the monocular runner to report progress and treat intentionally
  skipped trajectory saves as valid outcomes

commit.message:
parse alternative ichiran interpretations and repair romaji from kana

LangKit's disambiguation flow needs more than the old "primary tokens only" view
of ichiran output. We also found that some lower-ranked or alternative paths can
surface the right kana while leaving the romaji field unusable.

This teaches go-ichiran to return scored alternative segmentations and to
deterministically recover Latin romaji from kana when ichiran does not provide
a usable value.

∙ add AnalyzeOptions, AnalysisResult, and ScoredInterpretation
∙ plumb :limit through romanize* and parse full multi-interpretation output
∙ refactor parsing around parseWordEntry / parseWordEntriesToTokens
∙ parse gloss.alternative into token-level reading alternatives
∙ split slash-joined word-level romaji across alternatives
∙ fall back to robpike/nihongo for kana → romaji when romaji is empty or
  non-Latin
∙ keep primary token fields aligned with the selected-first alternative
∙ add parser tests for romaji repair and alternative romaji distribution

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Refactor/file Add build versioning, environment configuration, and file logging (#3)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
本次 PR 在构建/发布、运行时配置、日志记录与数据库初始化等方面进行了重构与增强，提升可部署性、可观测性与多架构发布能力。主要变更如下：

### 构建与发布
- CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml)：在 Go 构建时通过 -ldflags 注入版本信息（Version =
ci-${{ github.sha }}），便于在 CI 环境中追踪二进制版本。
- Release (.github/workflows/release.yml)：
  - 引入 Go 构建矩阵以并行构建 linux/amd64 与 linux/arm64，使用 matrix 驱动的 GOOS/GOARCH。
  - 生成带架构后缀的工件名（如 bot-linux-amd64），并通过 -X 将发布/标签版本嵌入二进制。
- 为产物生成确定性排序的 SHA256SUMS.txt，并改进 gh release create 时的 artifact
列表生成以保证顺序确定性。

### Bot（packages/bot）
- 新增公开变量 Version（默认 "dev"，可通过 -ldflags 覆盖）。
- 添加命令行标志 -version：打印 Version 后退出（在加载配置前）。
- 配置加载失败时将错误写入 stderr 并以非零退出码终止。
- 配置示例（packages/bot/config.example.yaml）：默认日志目录由 logs 改为
runtime/logs，存储目录由 storage 改为 runtime/storage。
- 新增 packages/bot/README.md，记录运行、配置与版本注入/校验（含 SHA256SUMS.txt）说明。

### Service（packages/service）
- 网络与启动
  - 新增环境变量 BIND_ADDR（默认 0.0.0.0:3000），服务监听地址可通过该变量配置；启动时记录实际绑定地址。
  - OpenAPI 文档 server 元数据由绝对 URL 改为相对路径 "/api"。
- 日志系统
- 引入基于文件的滚动日志（RollingFileAppender），使用 JSON 编码、按大小（10 MB）轮转、保留最多 5
个归档，同时保留 stdout 输出；日志目录由环境变量驱动。
- 数据库与模式迁移
  - Database::new 使用 SqliteConnectOptions 并显式启用 SQLite 外键支持。
- Database::init 新增 network_info
表初始化与迁移逻辑：若表缺失则创建；若存在但缺少外键约束，执行重命名旧表、重建含外键的新表、复制数据并删除旧表的迁移，确保
network_info.robot_uuid 外键指向 robots(uuid) 且 ON DELETE CASCADE。
- packages/service/schema.sql 为 network_info 添加 FOREIGN KEY (robot_uuid)
→ robots(uuid) ON DELETE CASCADE。
- 代码与格式调整
  - 多处日志/格式化字符串改为使用命名/捕获式格式（如 {err}、{robot_id}）。
  - 若干 async 函数添加 clippy 抑制属性（unused_async、needless_pass_by_value 等）。
- 若干内部构造函数/调用点调整了 Result/Ok(...) 的包装以统一返回/错误处理风格（如 Message
及相关指令构造处的同步修改）。

### 包与构建配置
- packages/service/Cargo.toml：增加 package
元数据（description、license、repository、publish = false）、profile.release
优化（lto、strip、codegen-units、opt-level）及 clippy lint 配置。

### 其他维护性改动
- .gitignore：新增常见编辑器/IDE 与操作系统临时文件规则（*.swp、.idea、.vscode、.DS_Store 等）。
- 示例/注释与文档更新：包括 packages/service/.env.example（新增 BIND_ADDR）、README 与
docs 文档扩充，多个文件的注释与错误/日志消息格式微调。


总体而言，本次提交围绕版本注入与多架构发布、运行时环境可配置性、结构化与文件化日志、以及数据库外键安全迁移进行了系统性改进，同时对若干内部实现与格式化进行了统一与修整，目标是提升可部署性、可维护性与运行时可观测性。

commit.message:
feat(platform): ship capability bootstrap and studio batch

Summary:
- add capability bootstrap, install doctoring, extension studio, and workflow branch-resume surfaces across backend and cockpit UI
- harden operator continuity with session-aware queued insights, scoped workflow run lookup, fail-soft cockpit refresh, and stale-safe studio async flows
- refresh implementation docs so the completed batch and next rolling queue match the current shipped app state

Rationale:
- make Seraph's existing skills, workflows, runbooks, starter packs, and MCP surfaces materially easier to activate, repair, and author
- keep the denser operator cockpit trustworthy by preserving partial data on refresh failures and tightening thread/replay continuity
- close review and subagent findings around optimistic outcomes, doc overclaims, path safety, and missing regressions before opening the aggregate PR

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_db_engine.py tests/test_catalog_api.py tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_skills_api.py tests/test_workflows.py

- cd docs && npm run build
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd frontend && npm test
- git diff --check

commit.message:
Refine heart-equivalence exactness and H0-prime reductions

This commit advances the `HeartEquivalence` development by adding the missing exactness
and reduction lemmas needed to move between the heart-level short-complex statements and
the `H0`/`H0prime` functorial exactness package.

The main additions are:
- a truncation-based isomorphism lemma for `truncLT` maps in the relevant `IsGE` range,
- vanishing and exactness lemmas for `H0prime` under the `IsGE 1` and heart-source cases,
- a collection of comparison lemmas linking `toH0primeHom`, `H0primeFunctor`, and the
  associated short complexes built from distinguished triangles,
- kernel-style exactness transport through `preadditiveCoyoneda`, and
- the heart-source reduction machinery needed to bootstrap `H0Functor` homologicality
  from the heart case to the general case.

The effect of these changes is to replace several ad hoc local proof blocks with reusable
private lemmas that isolate the octahedral/truncation manipulations and the exactness
arguments around `H0prime`. That leaves the higher-level homologicality argument in a much
cleaner shape and makes the remaining structure less brittle.

Verification:
- `lake build Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.HeartEquivalence`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Finish Euler form descent and simplify Corollary 1.3 assumptions

This commit finishes the remaining work in `EulerForm.lean` and wires the resulting
`EulerFormDescends` instance into the numerical manifold file in the more structural
way.

In `EulerForm.lean`, the Euler-form descent proof is now completed in both arguments.
The contravariant side is packaged through a `linearCoyoneda` homological instance,
and the covariant side is proved by running the long exact sequence on
`preadditiveYoneda`, extracting the exact rank identities, and summing them with the
alternating-sign cancellation lemma. This closes the last proof gap in the file and
removes all remaining `sorry`s there.

The covariant argument needs the shift functors to be `k`-linear in order to build the
connecting maps as `k`-linear maps, so the exported instance is now
`[∀ n : ℤ, Functor.Linear k (shiftFunctor C n)] -> EulerFormDescends k C`. That is the
right structural hypothesis here: `Linear k C` alone does not give shift-linearity in
this codebase.

In `NumericalStabilityManifold.lean`, the file now imports `EulerForm.lean` directly and
stops assuming `[EulerFormDescends k C]` explicitly in the final manifold theorem.
Instead, the theorem assumes the shift-linearity hypothesis, and the descent instance is
picked up by typeclass search from `EulerForm.lean`. This keeps the Corollary 1.3 side
closer to the real geometric input and removes an unnecessary hand-carried class
assumption from the file interface.

Verification:
- `lake env lean Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Triangulated/EulerForm.lean`
- `lake build Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.EulerForm`
- `lake build Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.NumericalStability`
- `lake build Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.NumericalStabilityManifold`
